meaning: A mathematical function used to keep the robot inside a safe set.
An optimization problem used to find the closest safe command to the nominal command.
Navigation where a robot follows natural-language instructions using visual perception.
A system that maps perception and language into robot actions.
A range sensor that measures distances using laser scanning.
ROS 2 communication policy settings such as reliability and durability.
One JSON record per line, useful for append-only logs.
Structured file format for large multimodal datasets.
The command proposed by the navigation backbone before safety filtering.
The command after the safety filter has applied constraints.
A safety margin. If h(x) is non-negative, the robot is inside the safe set.
Minimum allowed clearance around the robot.
Smallest safety margin over all obstacles at a timestep.
